title: Why x% of y Equals y% of x

md: Quick — what is $18\%$ of $50$? That looks unpleasant. But $50\%$ of $18$ is just *half of 18*, which is $9$.
And here's the secret: those two questions have the **same answer**. $18\%$ of $50$ *is* $9$. You're allowed to swap the numbers whenever it makes the arithmetic easier.

blocks.title: The claim
blocks.body: For any two numbers, $\;x\%\text{ of }y = y\%\text{ of }x.$

md: ### The proof is one line of translation
The word **"percent"** literally means "per hundred," and the word **"of"** means multiply. So translate each phrase straight into arithmetic.
$x\%$ of $y$ means: take $x$ out of every $100$, then apply it to $y$:
Now do the same for $y\%$ of $x$:
Both phrases collapsed to the **same expression** — because multiplication doesn't care about order ($xy = yx$). Two things equal to $\dfrac{xy}{100}$ are equal to each other:
On a $100$-square, "$x\%$ of $y$" and "$y\%$ of $x$" shade the *same rectangle* — one counts it by rows, the other by columns. Same shaded area, every time.

blocks.title: Your new mental-math superpower
blocks.body: Whenever a percent looks ugly, flip it. $4\%$ of $75$? Swap to $75\%$ of $4 = 3$. $12\%$ of $25$? That's $25\%$ of $12 = 3$. Pick whichever order your brain finds easy — the answer is identical.
